stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of NLP/Generative AI, specifically text-generation models.
The associated tags indicate its relevance within the transformers and llama ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model from Meta.
It is presented within the Hugging Face Benchmarks ecosystem, indicating community relevance.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation,' confirming its function as a generative AI model.

stories.title: Introducing Gemma 4 12B: a unified, encoder-free multimodal model
stories.author: olivier_lacombe
bullet_points: Introduction of Gemma 4 12B, a new unified, encoder-free multimodal model.
The model is specifically designed for high-performance deployment directly on local devices (laptops).
The focus is on bringing advanced multimodal intelligence to edge computing environments.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows running tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using both local and cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: This is your laptop… on AI
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: Big Tech is heavily emphasizing AI's transformative impact across all aspects of computing.
Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, strongly advocated for a fundamental shift in how laptops will be utilized.
The current developer conference season is heavily focused on the integration and future of AI in personal devices.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A startup named Antares has achieved criticality with a small modular nuclear reactor (SMR).
The reactor is currently in an early testing phase and is not yet capable of generating power.
This represents a significant step forward in the development of advanced nuclear energy technologies.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking direct investment access for SpaceX.
The exclusion also impacts major AI players like OpenAI and Anthropic.
This suggests a growing regulatory or structural hurdle for high-growth, non-traditional tech companies seeking passive investment.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Introduces Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework designed to inject repository-level context into Code Language Models.
Addresses the limitations of current methods (long inputs/RAG or full fine-tuning) which are costly and brittle to code evolution.
Achieves repository-specific knowledge injection with zero inference-time token overhead by generating LoRA adapters.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (AutoML).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, specifically inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and lack of hierarchical control.
Aims to enable sustained, long-horizon optimization for complex tasks like scientific discovery and ML engineering.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
This method addresses the limitations of current techniques (long inputs/per-repository fine-tuning) by injecting repository context without inference-time token overhead.
It offers a scalable and robust solution for code models dealing with software evolution and complex project dependencies.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a self-evolving, multi-agent framework designed for automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery (MLE).
Addresses key limitations in existing MLE agents, such as inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and lack of hierarchical control.
Positions the work within the growing trend of using LLM agents for complex, long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery and MLE engineering.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline shown is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting Apple silicon users.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of advanced proprietary models (like Claude) alongside open-source models.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB speaker has been demonstrated to be capable of infecting a PC without physical interaction.
The behavior in question is associated with the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The manufacturer of the device does not classify this behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in real-world humanoid robotics: the interface between high-level task planning and low-level whole-body control.
Introduces HANDOFF, a novel system designed to provide a compact, intuitive, and expressive command space for humanoid agents.
The method aims to overcome the limitation of existing controllers that require dense kinematic references difficult for planners to generate from task semantics.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed dynamically.
The research tackles the need for robots to switch between fast transit phases and slow, precise contact stages during manipulation.
Unlike previous methods that only change between fixed speeds, this work explores and enables controlled deceleration in VLA policies.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the process, not just the final output.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during human-AI co-editing.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into real-world drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential processing and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT achieves this by circumventing the need for traditional recurrent credit propagation, potentially improving parallelism and long-range dependency learning.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for reasoning language models.
It proposes RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work targets limitations in existing methods like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) used for Chain-of-Thought (CoT) generation.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: Introduces a novel method, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by parallel iterative denoising, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens from the denoising process are shown to be a valuable lookahead signal for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM weight conditioning during pre-training.
The PC module modifies the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows preconditioned weights to be merged back into the original architecture with zero inference overhead.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
TailLoR uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame for low-rank updates.
It employs a soft spectral penalty to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, focusing adaptation on 'long-tail' components.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in humanoid robotics: bridging the gap between high-level task planning and low-level whole-body control.
Introduces 'HANDOFF,' a novel, compact, and explicit interface designed to be intuitive and general for diverse manipulation skills.
Utilizes distilled Complementary Teachers, suggesting an advanced ML technique for robust control implementation.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection by focusing on the progressive, co-editing process between humans and AI.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants in professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential processing and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT achieves this by completely bypassing the need for traditional recurrent credit propagation, aiming to improve long-range association learning.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It proposes RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work targets models currently steered using algorithms like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) for Chain-of-Thought (CoT) generation.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage 'discarded' low-confidence tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded tokens serve as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
The technique aims to improve the retrieval process by surfacing salient entities early in the denoising trajectory.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the weight matrix's singular-value spectrum using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows for merging preconditioned weights back into the original architecture with no inference overhead.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the progressive human-AI co-editing process.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revisions.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants in real-world drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage discarded, low-confidence tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded tokens serve as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
The technique suggests that even uncertain predictions during the denoising process can surface salient entities early on.

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The research addresses the decoding efficiency bottleneck in Large Language Models (LLMs) during long-context inference.
It tackles the common trade-off between speed and quality in existing sparse attention methods.
The proposed 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing' aims to improve efficiency without significant quality degradation.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the effectiveness of 'prompt skills' (like instructing an LLM to act as a Popperian falsificationist) in improving code generation by LLMs.
A key concern raised is that performance gains are often measured using LLM-as-a-judge systems, which suffer from known biases (positional, self-preference, stylistic).
The research questions whether the observed improvements in code quality are genuinely due to the prompting technique or artifacts of the evaluation methodology.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as an alternative to explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs.
Traditional textual CoT is limited because it forces complex, semantic computation through a slow, discrete token stream.
Latent reasoning aims to offer a higher-bandwidth method for intermediate computation, potentially improving reasoning efficiency.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: USAD 2.0 introduces a universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs for modern LLMs.
The research addresses limitations in existing multi-domain audio approaches (like USAD and SPEAR) regarding coverage and evaluation.
The model integrates knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods to improve audio understanding.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of using LLMs to simulate user stances in online discussions.
It proposes 'counterfactual context revision' as a framework to audit LLM stance simulation.
The study investigates whether LLM simulations accurately reflect user beliefs or are overly sensitive to minor context changes.

stories.title: Gemma 4 QAT models: Optimizing compression for mobile and laptop efficiency
stories.author: theanonymousone
bullet_points: The story focuses on Gemma 4 Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) models.
The primary goal is optimizing model compression for deployment on mobile and laptop devices.
This addresses the critical industry need for efficient, on-device AI inference.

stories.title: New method turns ocean water into drinking water, without waste
stories.author: speckx
bullet_points: A new method for desalination has been developed.
The process claims to convert ocean water into potable drinking water.
A key feature of the method is that it operates without generating waste.

stories.title: KV cache quantization: what FP8/INT8 K and V actually buy you, and where they break
stories.author: tech_nuggets
bullet_points: The article discusses the impact of quantizing Key (K) and Value (V) caches using FP8/INT8 formats.
Quantization can significantly reduce attention state memory usage by about 50%.
A key drawback is that quantization shifts the target model's logit distribution, potentially negating gains from speculative decoding.

stories.title: Your AI Agent Drifted Last Night and You Didn't Notice
stories.author: saurav_bhattacharya
bullet_points: Highlights the critical, often unseen problem of AI agent drift.
Addresses the gap between successful testing (CI/staging) and real-world performance failure.
Indicates a need for monitoring solutions beyond standard testing pipelines for deployed AI agents.
